title: cleistocalyx cerasoides
intro: Cleistocalyx cerasoides, also known as the rose-apple tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of Asia, particularly in India, China, and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cleistocalyx cerasoides include relief from fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, and it is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for various herbal preparations.
